commands.repair_reason: - [P1] This PR needs maintainer compatibility judgment and stronger transport proof rather than an automated repair because no discrete code defect was found in the submitted patch.; Cleared: The diff changes message-tool execution routing but does not add dependencies, secrets handling, CI, downloads, package scripts, or other supply-chain surfaces. (sha=7bfe23b3fd61921436a11e200b8c9fef4ebead3b)
commands.review_summary:
The branch adds automatic-mode message-tool guidance, rejects implicit text-only current-source `message/send` calls in the outbound runner, and adds focused tests for the rejected implicit path plus explicit-target and structured-payload escape paths.

PR surface: Source +43, Tests +76. Total +119 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. source inspection and the supplied Feishu logs give a credible reproduction shape: automatic streaming-card delivery can coexist with a model `message/send` path that bypasses the source reply dispatcher. I did not independently run a live Feishu current-main repro in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] This PR needs maintainer compatibility judgment and stronger transport proof rather than an automated repair because no discrete code defect was found in the submitted patch.

target.title: fix(message): keep automatic replies out of implicit sends
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Prevent implicit, text-only `message(action="send")` calls from defaulting to the current source conversation when the turn is already using `sourceReplyDeliveryMode: "automatic"`.
- Keep the automatic source reply dispatcher as the owner of normal visible replies, so Feishu/Lark streaming-card turns do not split into an early direct post plus a final card.

Why does this matter now?

- Codex-backed OpenClaw runs can expose the shared `message` tool. When the model uses that tool for ordinary visible text during automatic delivery, the send path bypasses the active source reply dispatcher.
- In a real Feishu p2p setup with streaming cards enabled, this produced exactly the confusing UX reported by the user: the initial answer rendered as a normal Feishu message, while only the final/footer portion used the streaming card.

What is the intended outcome?

- In automatic source delivery, text-only current-conversation replies should be delivered by the normal assistant final response path.
- `message/send` remains available for explicit out-of-band targets and for structured payloads such as media, presentations, or interactive/card payloads.

What is intentionally out of scope?

- This PR does not change `message_tool_only` semantics.
- This PR does not rewrite Feishu streaming-card merge/update behavior.
- This PR does not change explicit Feishu card sends such as the case discussed in #53486.

What does success look like?

- A model cannot accidentally turn an automatic Feishu/Lark streaming reply into a direct `post` by calling implicit text-only `message/send` to the current source conversation.
- Explicit-target sends and non-text payload sends continue to work.

What should reviewers focus on?

- Whether the implicit-send rejection boundary is narrow enough.
- Whether the message tool guidance is clear without making legitimate explicit sends harder.
- Whether the regression tests cover the three relevant branches: reject implicit text, allow explicit target, allow structured payload.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Closes #

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Related #53486
Related #84486

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No maintainer request. This came from a real OpenClaw + Feishu + Codex user report while debugging streaming-card delivery.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: In automatic source delivery, an implicit text-only `message(action="send")` to the current Feishu/Lark source conversation can bypass the streaming-card reply dispatcher and render as a normal Feishu `post`. This PR codifies the local mitigation by rejecting that implicit current-source text send and telling the model to reply normally instead.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw `2026.5.27 (27ae826)` on macOS, Feishu/Lark p2p channel over websocket, Codex runtime, `messages.visibleReplies=automatic`, `channels.feishu.streaming=true`, `channels.feishu.footer.elapsed=true`, `channels.feishu.footer.status=true`. The source branch itself was validated with local tests; the live Feishu run used the installed OpenClaw package with `agents.main.tools.deny` containing `message` as an equivalent mitigation for the same failure mode.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Reproduced the split delivery on the live Feishu setup: reply mode resolved as streaming, but the model used `message(action="send")` and the first visible answer appeared as a normal Feishu post; the final completion/footer appeared in a streaming card.
  2. Applied the local mitigation that this PR turns into a runtime policy: `openclaw config set messages.visibleReplies automatic`, `openclaw config set channels.feishu.streaming true`, `openclaw config set channels.feishu.footer.elapsed true`, `openclaw config set channels.feishu.footer.status true`, and added `message` to `agents.main.tools.deny`.
  3. Restarted the gateway and sent another Feishu p2p prompt from the same account.
  4. Captured live OpenClaw CLI/log output with `openclaw --version`, `openclaw config get ...`, `openclaw config get agents.list`, and `openclaw logs --plain --limit 80 --max-bytes 200000`.
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output): Copied live terminal output and redacted runtime log excerpt from the real Feishu p2p setup:

```text
$ openclaw --version
OpenClaw 2026.5.27 (27ae826)

$ openclaw config get messages.visibleReplies
automatic
$ openclaw config get channels.feishu.streaming
true
$ openclaw config get channels.feishu.footer.elapsed
true
$ openclaw config get channels.feishu.footer.status
true
$ openclaw config get agents.list
[
  {
    "id": "main",
    "tools": {
      "deny": ["...", "message"]
    }
  }
]

2026-05-30T11:53:28.337+08:00 info feishu/card/reply-dispatcher ... reply mode resolved (effectiveReplyMode=auto, replyMode=streaming, chatType=p2p)
2026-05-30T11:53:28.338+08:00 info feishu/card/reply-dispatcher ... footer config resolved (... useStreamingCards=true, rawFooter={"elapsed":true,"status":true}, resolvedFooter={"status":true,"elapsed":true,...})
2026-05-30T11:53:29.511+08:00 info agents/tool-policy ... tool policy removed 1 tool(s) via agents.main.tools.deny: message; matched message
2026-05-30T11:55:02.696+08:00 info feishu/card/streaming ... phase transition (from=idle, to=creating, source=ensureCardCreated)
2026-05-30T11:55:03.688+08:00 info feishu/card/streaming ... sent CardKit card (messageId=redacted)
2026-05-30T11:55:03.693+08:00 info feishu/card/streaming ... phase transition (from=streaming, to=completed, source=onIdle, reason=normal)
2026-05-30T11:55:03.696+08:00 info channels/feishu ... dispatch complete (queuedFinal=true, replies=1)
2026-05-30T11:55:04.329+08:00 info feishu/card/streaming ... reply completed, card finalized (elapsedMs=95990, isCardKit=true)
```

- Observed result after fix: With the implicit current-source message tool path removed in the live Feishu setup, the next reply rendered through the Feishu CardKit streaming path as a single finalized streaming card (`queuedFinal=true, replies=1`) instead of the previous post-plus-card split. The code in this PR applies the same boundary without globally disabling `message`: implicit current-source text sends are rejected only in automatic mode, while explicit target sends and structured payload sends remain allowed.
- What was not tested: I did not replace the live installed Feishu gateway package with this source checkout. The exact source patch was validated through targeted runner/tool tests and typechecking; the live Feishu evidence validates the failure mode and the equivalent runtime mitigation on the real user setup.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The Feishu chat IDs, message IDs, and user IDs are redacted. The user-facing Feishu screenshot was observed during debugging, but this PR body uses copied terminal/log output rather than embedding private chat screenshots.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before removing the implicit message-tool path, the same live setup logged `replyMode=streaming` / `useStreamingCards=true`, then the visible answer was sent through the direct message path as a Feishu post; the UI showed an initial normal Feishu bubble followed by the final streaming-card footer. That before/after comparison is why this PR targets only implicit text-only current-source sends in automatic delivery.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/message-tool.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts src/infra/outbound/message-action-runner.send-validation.test.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:test:src`
- `git diff --check`
- `codex review --base origin/main`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

- Added a send-validation test that rejects automatic-mode implicit text-only sends to the current source conversation.
- Added coverage that explicit-target sends still use the normal outbound path.
- Added coverage that implicit structured/media sends are still allowed.
- Updated message-tool description coverage so automatic source delivery tells the model to reply normally for visible current-source text.

What failed before this fix, if known?

- The live Feishu setup could enter streaming-card mode and still emit the early visible answer as a normal Feishu post when the model used implicit `message/send` for the current-source text reply.

If no test was added, why not?

- Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. In automatic source delivery, implicit text-only `message/send` to the current source conversation now fails fast instead of sending a duplicate/direct visible reply.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes, tool execution behavior changes narrowly for `message/send` when all of these are true: automatic source delivery, current source context exists, no explicit target, and text-only payload.

What is the highest-risk area?

Accidentally blocking legitimate `message/send` calls that should still be allowed.

How is that risk mitigated?

The guard is limited to automatic source delivery, requires a current source channel, and only applies when there is no explicit target and no structured payload. Tests cover explicit-target and media payload escape paths.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Ready for maintainer review after the real-behavior-proof gate and CI complete.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

Waiting on CI and maintainer judgment about whether the live mitigation proof is sufficient or if they want a full source-checkout Feishu gateway run.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

The initial Real behavior proof gate failure was addressed by rewriting the section into the official required field format with copied live output.

## AI-assisted

- [x] AI-assisted PR.
- [x] I reviewed the code path and understand the change.
- [x] Local Codex review was run before opening this PR.
target.author: qiangu
